title: Kenya IT1 Individual Resident Return — Employed Resident Individual
description: The Kenya Revenue Authority's (KRA) "IT1_Individual_Resident_Return" Excel workbook (internal version 18.0.1, matching the current live filename), the offline data-entry template Kenyan resident individuals complete and later upload (as an XML export) through the iTax portal (itax.kra.go.ke) to file their annual income tax return. Closes Kenya's Taxes vertical (3 of 6 verticals; Business Formation and National ID are already modelled via ke/brs/cr1-application-to-register-a-company and ke/nrb/application-for-identity-card). This v1.0.0 models the employed-resident-individual filing pathway only — a taxpayer whose sole income is employment income (PAYE), with no business/farming, partnership, estate/trust, rental, car-benefit, mortgage, Home Ownership Savings Plan, life-insurance, commercial-vehicle, foreign/DTAA, or spousal-joint income to declare — covering five of the workbook's 34 sheets: A_Basic_Info (return identification, the nine other-income/benefit routing declarations, the disability exemption certificate, and optional bank details), F_Employment_Income (per-employer employment income detail, bounded to 2 employers as the sheet itself provides), M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted (per-employer PAYE-already-deducted detail, likewise bounded to 2 employers), and T_Tax_Computation (the two genuine free-text inputs relevant to this pathway — pension contribution and personal relief — plus the workbook's own computed rollup lines through to the final tax due/refund figure). The sixth named sheet, T_Income_Computation_Self, is a pure formula rollup of the excluded Business/Farming/Rental/Interest/Commission/Other income categories and contributes zero raw-entry fields to this pathway — a disclosed structural finding, not an omission; see VERIFICATION.md. Out of scope for this v1.0.0 (each a candidate for a future companion schema): the Profit & Loss/Balance Sheet/Stock/Capital Allowances sheets (B, C, D, E1, E2, E) for self-employed or business income; Partnership income (G); Estate/Trust income (H); Car Benefit detail (I); Mortgage interest (J); Home Ownership Savings Plan (K); Life Insurance Relief (L); Installment Tax, Withholding Tax, Advance Tax, IT Payment, and DTAA credit schedules (N, O, P, Q, R); prior-year losses (S); and the entire spousal/"Wife" income-declaration pathway (T_Income_Computation_Wife and every Wife-suffixed field across the other sheets), since this workbook supports joint/separate spousal filing as a distinct, materially larger surface. This document describes the workbook only; it does not file a return on the taxpayer's behalf, and the live iTax portal (itax.kra.go.ke) and KRA's own current publications are always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Republic of Kenya or the Kenya Revenue Authority.

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2535/GOV-2533. Closes Kenya's Taxes vertical (3 of 6; Business Formation and National ID already modelled). This exact candidate was flagged across three prior cycles (GOV-2500/GOV-2507/GOV-2517) and was already CATALOG.md's own confirmed-strong, corrected-in-place Known Gaps entry (item 21): a same-day GOV-2507 'reversed to dead end' claim (based on one genuinely MS-Office-encrypted sibling file) was itself corrected by GOV-2517, which identified two live, unauthenticated, unencrypted siblings on kra.go.ke. This document independently re-fetched the cited sibling (IT1_Individual_Resident_Return_18.0.1latest.xls) fresh via a plain HTTPS GET: HTTP 200, 3,323,904 bytes, sha256:7c85241bbabc797b9e3c6b708209a32866c4ec9131f9d845d0b87c9734e939e2 — exactly matching the byte size and hash already cited going into this cycle. Opened cleanly with the xlsx npm package (BIFF/Excel-97-2003 format, no password prompt), confirming all 34 sheets by name (Data, Read Me, Errors, Validations, ValidationList, Macros_Disabled, ImportCsv, and the lettered content sheets A_Basic_Info through T_Tax_Computation, plus Sheet1) — an exact match to this cycle's own pre-stated sheet list. This also resolves CATALOG.md's own standing 'suspect' flag on the workbook's version number: GOV-2500 had cited '19.0.3/March 2026' and GOV-2517 could not corroborate it anywhere; this cycle's own extraction of cell A_Basic_Info!I1 reads '18.0.1' — matching the live filename itself ('...18.0.1latest.xls') — so '19.0.3' appears to have been a citation error in the GOV-2500 cycle, not a real prior edition; treat '18.0.1' as the confirmed current version. See VERIFICATION.md for the full sheet-by-sheet extraction method (raw sheet_to_json row/column dumps cross-correlated against the workbook's own internal Defined Names, the Validations and ValidationList reference sheets for mandatory/format rules, and cell-formula inspection via {cellFormula:true} to distinguish genuine free-text input cells from macro/formula-computed rollups), every scoping and disclosure judgment call (the Type of Return dropdown's two values sourced from the Data sheet's own ReturnType named range; the KRA-PIN 11-character pattern combining the Validations sheet's own MinLength/MaxLength=11 with Kenya's well-known public PIN format, disclosed as a supplementary convention since the workbook's validatePIN() check is a compiled macro with no extractable literal regex; the Tax Slabs annotation embedded as plain cell text at T_Tax_Computation!F15, cited but not reproduced as an executable formula since the actual computation runs through an external, non-extractable VBA function; and the finding that T_Income_Computation_Self contributes zero fields to this pathway), and the conformance run writeup (2 valid fixtures, 0 errors each; 5 mutation-control fixtures, exactly 1 error each).

fields.name: pin
fields.label: Personal Identification Number *
fields.description: The taxpayer's own KRA Personal Identification Number (PIN).
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B3 (named range RetInf.PIN), label A3 — Validations sheet row 1: MinLength=11, MaxLength=11, Validation Rule2 validatePIN(). The 11-character letter+9-digit+letter pattern below reflects Kenya's well-known public KRA PIN format combined with the sourced 11-character length constraint; the workbook's own validatePIN() check is a compiled macro with no extractable literal regex, so this pattern is a disclosed supplementary convention, not literally read out of the workbook — see VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: typeOfReturn
fields.label: Type of Return *
fields.description: Whether this is an original or an amended return.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B4 (named range RetInf.RetType), label A4 — dropdown sourced from Data!D19:D20 (named range ReturnType): "Original", "Amended".

fields.name: returnPeriodFrom
fields.label: Return Period From *
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B5 (named range RetInf.RetStartDate), label A5.

fields.name: returnPeriodTo
fields.label: Return Period To *
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B6 (named range RetInf.RetEndDate), label A6.

fields.name: hasOtherIncomeThanEmployment
fields.label: Do you have any income other than employment income? *
fields.description: Routes to the Business/Farming/Rental/Interest/Commission/Other income computation (B_Profit_Loss_Account_Self, C_Balance_Sheet, D_Stock_Analysis, E1/E2 Capital Allowances, and T_Income_Computation_Self's own six income-category columns), all out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B7 (named range RetInf.DeclareSelfBusIncome), label A7.

fields.name: hasPartnershipIncome
fields.label: Do you have partnership income? *
fields.description: Routes to G_Partnership_Income, out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B8 (named range RetInf.dispSec.PartnershipIncome), label A8.

fields.name: hasEstateTrustIncome
fields.label: Do you have estate trust income? *
fields.description: Routes to H_Estate_Trust_Income, out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B9 (named range dispSec.EstateTrustIncome), label A9.

fields.name: employerProvidedCar
fields.label: Has your employer provided you with a car? *
fields.description: Routes to I_Computation_of_Car_Benefit, out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0 (the corresponding "Value of Car Benefit" rollup on F_Employment_Income is consequently always zero here — see that field's own description).
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B10 (named range dispSec.CarBenefit), label A10.

fields.name: hasMortgage
fields.label: Do you have a mortgage? *
fields.description: Routes to J_Computation_of_Mortgage, out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B11 (named range dispSec.Mortgage), label A11.

fields.name: hasHomeOwnershipSavingsPlan
fields.label: Do you have a Home Ownership Savings Plan? *
fields.description: Routes to K_Home_Ownership_Saving_Plan, out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B12 (named range dispSec.OwnershipSaving), label A12.

fields.name: hasLifeInsurancePolicy
fields.label: Do you have a life insurance policy? *
fields.description: Routes to L_Computation_of_Insu_Relief, out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B13 (named range dispSec.InsuRelief), label A13.

fields.name: hasCommercialVehicle
fields.label: Do you have a commercial vehicle? *
fields.description: Routes to P_Advance_Tax_Credits (Advance Tax on Commercial Vehicle), out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B14 (named range dispSec.AdvanceTax), label A14.

fields.name: earnsForeignIncome
fields.label: Do you earn any income from a foreign country? *
fields.description: Routes to R_DTAA_Credits (double-taxation-agreement relief), out of scope for this employed-only v1.0.0.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B15 (named range dispSec.DTAA), label A15.

fields.name: hasDisabilityExemptionCertificate
fields.label: Have you been issued the Exemption Certificate for disability? *
fields.description: Gates the Section A Part 6 disability exemption certificate list below, which remains in scope (it lives on A_Basic_Info itself, not a separate excluded sheet). The resulting relief amount on T_Tax_Computation (line 12.3, "Allowable Tax Exemption in case of Person with Disability") is a macro-computed value this schema does not model as a field — see VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B16 (named range RetInf.DeclareExemptionCerti), label A16.

fields.name: disabilityExemptionCertificateNumber1
fields.label: Exemption Certificate Number
fields.description: First of up to 2 disability exemption certificates (the sheet itself provides exactly 2 list rows). Required once hasDisabilityExemptionCertificate is true.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!A68 (named range ExemptCerti.ListS, row 1 of 2), header at A67.

fields.name: disabilityExemptionCertificateDateFrom1
fields.label: Date From
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B68 (named range ExemptCerti.ListS, row 1 of 2), header at B67.

fields.name: disabilityExemptionCertificateDateTo1
fields.label: Date To
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!C68 (named range ExemptCerti.ListS, row 1 of 2), header at C67.

fields.name: disabilityExemptionCertificateNumber2
fields.label: Exemption Certificate Number
fields.description: Second of up to 2 disability exemption certificates, when the taxpayer holds more than one.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!A69 (named range ExemptCerti.ListS, row 2 of 2).

fields.name: disabilityExemptionCertificateDateFrom2
fields.label: Date From
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B69 (named range ExemptCerti.ListS, row 2 of 2).

fields.name: disabilityExemptionCertificateDateTo2
fields.label: Date To
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!C69 (named range ExemptCerti.ListS, row 2 of 2).

fields.name: wantsToDeclareWifeIncome
fields.label: Do you want to declare Wife's Income? *
fields.description: Routes to the entire spousal-declaration pathway (Wife's PIN, T_Income_Computation_Wife, and every Wife-suffixed field across A_Basic_Info/F_Employment_Income/M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted/T_Tax_Computation) — out of scope for this v1.0.0, a materially larger companion-schema candidate given this workbook's joint/separate spousal filing support.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B17 (named range RetInf.DeclareWifeIncome), label A17.

fields.name: bankName
fields.label: Bank Name
fields.description: Optional bank details (Section A Part 2), used for any refund payment. Not conditioned on any other declaration — the source marks these fields Mandatory=N unconditionally.
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!D23 (named range BankDtl.BankNameS), label A23 — Validations sheet: MaxLength=15.

fields.name: branchName
fields.label: Branch Name
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!D24 (named range BankDtl.BranchNameS), label A24 — Validations sheet: MaxLength=15.

fields.name: bankCity
fields.label: City
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B25 (named range BankDtl.CityS), label A25 — Validations sheet: MaxLength=15, validateAlphabetOnly.

fields.name: accountHolderName
fields.label: Account Holder's Name
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B26 (named range BankDtl.AccNameS), label A26 — Validations sheet: MaxLength=15.

fields.name: accountNumber
fields.label: Account Number
fields.sourceRef: A_Basic_Info!B27 (named range BankDtl.AccNumberS), label A27 — Validations sheet: MaxLength=15.

fields.name: employer1PIN
fields.label: PIN of Employer
fields.description: First employer, of up to 2 the sheet itself provides rows for.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!A3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at A2.

fields.name: employer1Name
fields.label: Name of Employer
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!B3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at B2.

fields.name: employer1GrossPay
fields.label: Gross Pay (Ksh) (A)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!C3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at C2.

fields.name: employer1AllowancesAndBenefits
fields.label: Allowances and Benefits from Employment (Other than Car and Housing) (Ksh) (B)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!D3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at D2.

fields.name: employer1NetValueOfHousing
fields.label: Net Value of Housing (Ksh) (D)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!F3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at F2.

fields.name: employer1PensionInExcessOf300000
fields.label: Pension if in excess of 300,000 (Ksh) (E)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!G3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at G2.

fields.name: employer1TotalEmploymentIncome
fields.label: Total Employment Income (Ksh) (F) = (A+B+C+D+E)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as Gross Pay + Allowances/Benefits + Value of Car Benefit + Net Housing + Pension excess, per row; not entered directly. "Value of Car Benefit" (component C, column E on the source) is itself pulled from I_Computation_of_Car_Benefit, out of scope here, so it is always zero for this employed-only pathway.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H3 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 1 of 2), header at H2 — formula =IF(COUNTBLANK(C3:G3)=5,"",SUM(C3:G3)).

fields.name: employer2PIN
fields.label: PIN of Employer
fields.description: Second employer, of up to 2 the sheet itself provides rows for.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!A4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at A2.

fields.name: employer2Name
fields.label: Name of Employer
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!B4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at B2.

fields.name: employer2GrossPay
fields.label: Gross Pay (Ksh) (A)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!C4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at C2.

fields.name: employer2AllowancesAndBenefits
fields.label: Allowances and Benefits from Employment (Other than Car and Housing) (Ksh) (B)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!D4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at D2.

fields.name: employer2NetValueOfHousing
fields.label: Net Value of Housing (Ksh) (D)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!F4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at F2.

fields.name: employer2PensionInExcessOf300000
fields.label: Pension if in excess of 300,000 (Ksh) (E)
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!G4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at G2.

fields.name: employer2TotalEmploymentIncome
fields.label: Total Employment Income (Ksh) (F) = (A+B+C+D+E)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as Gross Pay + Allowances/Benefits + Value of Car Benefit + Net Housing + Pension excess, per row; not entered directly. "Value of Car Benefit" (component C, column E on the source) is itself pulled from I_Computation_of_Car_Benefit, out of scope here, so it is always zero for this employed-only pathway.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H4 (named range EmpIncome.ListS row 2 of 2), header at H2 — formula =IF(COUNTBLANK(C4:G4)=5,"",SUM(C4:G4)).

fields.name: totalEmploymentIncomeAllEmployers
fields.label: Total
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as the sum of Total Employment Income across all declared employers (both rows); not entered directly.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H5, label A5 — formula =SUM(H3:OFFSET(EmpIncome.ListSTO,-1,0)).

fields.name: employmentIncomeJanuaryToMarch
fields.label: January - March: Income
fields.description: The portion of total employment income earned in the January-March period, entered directly (used to apply the correct sub-period tax computation). The workbook's own validateJanDecIncome2020 check requires this plus employmentIncomeAprilToDecember to sum to totalEmploymentIncomeAllEmployers; that arithmetic constraint is disclosed here and in VERIFICATION.md rather than encoded, since the spec's crossFieldValidation grammar does not support a multi-field sum comparison.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H6 (named range EmpIncome.ListSTOJANMAR), label A6 — Validations sheet row 40: Mandatory Y.

fields.name: pensionJanuaryToMarch
fields.label: January - March: Pension
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H7 (named range EmpIncome.ListSPENSJANMAR), label A7 — Validations sheet row 42: Mandatory Y.

fields.name: employmentIncomeAprilToDecember
fields.label: April-December: Income
fields.description: The portion of total employment income earned in the April-December period. See employmentIncomeJanuaryToMarch's description for the disclosed sum constraint against totalEmploymentIncomeAllEmployers.
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H8 (named range EmpIncome.ListSTOAPRDEC), label A8 — Validations sheet row 40: Mandatory Y.

fields.name: pensionAprilToDecember
fields.label: April - December: Pension
fields.sourceRef: F_Employment_Income!H9 (named range EmpIncome.ListSTOJanDecPensionSum's sibling input EmpIncome.ListSPENSAPRDEC), label A9 — Validations sheet row 42: Mandatory Y.

fields.name: payeEmployer1PIN
fields.label: PIN of Employer
fields.description: First employer, of up to 2 the sheet itself provides rows for. Independent of F_Employment_Income's own employer list (the source workbook does not structurally link the two by PIN), though in practice they describe the same employer(s).
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!A3 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 1 of 2), header at A2.

fields.name: payeEmployer1Name
fields.label: Name of Employer
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!B3 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 1 of 2), header at B2.

fields.name: payeEmployer1TaxableSalary
fields.label: Taxable Salary (Ksh)
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!C3 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 1 of 2), header at C2.

fields.name: payeEmployer1TaxPayableOnTaxableSalary
fields.label: Tax Payable on Taxable Salary (Ksh) (A)
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!D3 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 1 of 2), header at D2.

fields.name: payeEmployer1AmountOfTaxDeductedPAYE
fields.label: Amount of Tax Deducted (PAYE) (Ksh) (B)
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!E3 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 1 of 2), header at E2.

fields.name: payeEmployer1AmountOfTaxPayableOrRefundable
fields.label: Amount of Tax Payable or Refundable (PAYE) (Ksh) (C) = (A-B)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as Tax Payable on Taxable Salary minus Amount of Tax Deducted (PAYE); not entered directly. Can be negative (a refundable amount).
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!F3 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 1 of 2), header at F2 — formula =IF(D3="","",IF(E3="","",ROUND(D3,2)-ROUND(E3,2))).

fields.name: payeEmployer2PIN
fields.label: PIN of Employer
fields.description: Second employer, of up to 2 the sheet itself provides rows for. Independent of F_Employment_Income's own employer list (the source workbook does not structurally link the two by PIN), though in practice they describe the same employer(s).
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!A4 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 2 of 2), header at A2.

fields.name: payeEmployer2Name
fields.label: Name of Employer
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!B4 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 2 of 2), header at B2.

fields.name: payeEmployer2TaxableSalary
fields.label: Taxable Salary (Ksh)
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!C4 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 2 of 2), header at C2.

fields.name: payeEmployer2TaxPayableOnTaxableSalary
fields.label: Tax Payable on Taxable Salary (Ksh) (A)
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!D4 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 2 of 2), header at D2.

fields.name: payeEmployer2AmountOfTaxDeductedPAYE
fields.label: Amount of Tax Deducted (PAYE) (Ksh) (B)
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!E4 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 2 of 2), header at E2.

fields.name: payeEmployer2AmountOfTaxPayableOrRefundable
fields.label: Amount of Tax Payable or Refundable (PAYE) (Ksh) (C) = (A-B)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as Tax Payable on Taxable Salary minus Amount of Tax Deducted (PAYE); not entered directly. Can be negative (a refundable amount).
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!F4 (named range PayeDed.ListS row 2 of 2), header at F2 — formula =IF(D4="","",IF(E4="","",ROUND(D4,2)-ROUND(E4,2))).

fields.name: totalAmountOfTaxDeductedPAYESelf
fields.label: Total
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as the sum of Amount of Tax Deducted (PAYE) across all declared employers; not entered directly. Feeds T_Tax_Computation line 13.1 (PAYE Deducted from Salary).
fields.sourceRef: M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted!E5, label A5 — formula =SUM(E3:OFFSET(PayeDed.ListSTO,-1,0)).

fields.name: pensionContributionSelf
fields.label: Defined / Pension Contribution
fields.description: The taxpayer's registered-pension-scheme contribution for the year, entered directly. Feeds Total Deduction (line 11) against employment income. The workbook does not state a statutory cap in a plain-text cell near this input, so none is asserted here.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C5 (named range DedDtl.PensionS), line 11.1 label at B5 — Validations sheet: Mandatory N.

fields.name: personalReliefSelf
fields.label: Personal Relief
fields.description: The taxpayer's personal relief amount, entered directly (not auto-computed by any in-sheet formula). Cell F18 carries a developer note reading "Keep it 13,944 but make it configurable" — disclosed here as an internal implementation note found in the workbook, not asserted as the current statutory relief amount (Kenya's statutory personal relief has changed over time; this schema does not invent or default a value). Feeds Total of Tax Payable Less Reliefs and Exemptions (line 12).
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C18 (named range TaxComp.PersonalReliefS), line 12.6 label at B18 — Validations sheet: Mandatory N.

fields.name: totalDeductionSelf
fields.label: Total Deduction (11.1+ Higher value of 11.2 or 11.3)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as Pension Contribution plus the higher of Mortgage Interest or Home Ownership Savings Plan deposit (both out of scope here, so effectively equal to pensionContributionSelf for this pathway); not entered directly.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C3, line 11 label at B3 — formula =IF(C6>C7,C5+C6,C5+C7).

fields.name: employmentIncomeSelfComputed
fields.label: Employment Income ("Total Taxable Employment Income" from F_Employement_Income)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as a direct copy of totalEmploymentIncomeAllEmployers from F_Employment_Income; not entered directly. Feeds Net Taxable Income (line 12.4).
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C11, line 12.1 label at B11 — formula =EmpIncome.ListSTO.

fields.name: taxPayableLessReliefsAndExemptionsSelf
fields.label: Total of Tax Payable Less Reliefs and Exemptions (12.5 - 12.6 - 12.7)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as Tax on Taxable Income minus Personal Relief minus Insurance Relief (the latter out of scope here), floored at zero; not entered directly.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C9, line 12 label at B9 — formula references TaxComp.TaxPayableS, TaxComp.PersonalReliefS, TaxComp.InsReliefDtlsListSTO.

fields.name: netTaxableIncomeSelf
fields.label: Net Taxable Income (12.1+ 12.2+Line Number 9 (Consolidated) - 11-12.3)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook from Employment Income, Estate/Trust income (out of scope, always zero here), the consolidated non-employment computation from T_Income_Computation_Self (always zero for this employed-only pathway — see this document's own top-level description), Total Deduction, and the disability exemption amount (out of scope as a field, see hasDisabilityExemptionCertificate); not entered directly.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C14, line 12.4 label at B14 — formula references TaxComp.EmpIncomeListSTO, TaxComp.TaxbleIncomeEstateS, TaxComp.ChargeableIncomeS, DedDtl.TotalDeductionS, TaxComp.ExemptedAmtS.

fields.name: taxOnTaxableIncomeSelf
fields.label: Tax on Taxable Income (12.4 *applicable rates)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook by applying Kenya's individual income tax bands to Net Taxable Income, via an external VBA macro function (getTotalTaxPayble) that is not present as a literal, reproducible spreadsheet formula — per this registry's convention, this schema does not attempt to reimplement that computation. The workbook does document the underlying rate bands as plain cell text (see sourceRef); not entered directly.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C15, line 12.5 label at B15, formula = getTotalTaxPayble(TaxComp.NetTaxableIncomeS,SecA.RtnYear) — rate bands documented at F15: "Tax Slabs: 0 to 1,21,968 (10%); 1,21,969 to 2,36,880 (15%); 2,36,881 to 3,51,792 (20%); 3,51,793 to 4,66,704 (25%); Over 4,66,704 (30%)" (figures as printed in the workbook, Indian-style digit grouping; i.e. KES 121,968 / 236,880 / 351,792 / 466,704).

fields.name: payeDeductedFromSalarySelfComputed
fields.label: PAYE Deducted from Salary (Total of "Amount of Tax Deducted (PAYE)" from M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as a direct copy of totalAmountOfTaxDeductedPAYESelf from M_Details_of_PAYE_Deducted; not entered directly. Feeds Tax Credits (line 13).
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C22, line 13.1 label at B22 — formula =PayeDed.ListSTO.

fields.name: taxCreditsTotalSelf
fields.label: Tax Credits (13.1+13.2+13.3+13.4+13.5+13.6+13.7+13.8)
fields.description: Computed by the workbook as the sum of lines 13.1 through 13.8; for this employed-only pathway every component except 13.1 (PAYE Deducted from Salary) is out of scope and zero (estate/trust credit, installment tax, withholding tax, advance tax on commercial vehicle, income tax paid in advance, DTAA credits, and rental withholding tax), so this reduces to payeDeductedFromSalarySelfComputed here; not entered directly.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C21, line 13 label at B21 — formula =SUM(C22:C29).

fields.name: taxDueOrRefundDueSelf
fields.label: Tax Due / (Refund Due) ((12 (Self) + 12 (Wife) - 13(Self) - 13 (Wife))
fields.description: The workbook's own bottom-line filing outcome, computed as Total of Tax Payable Less Reliefs and Exemptions minus Tax Credits (the Wife terms are always zero for this employed-only, non-joint-filing pathway); not entered directly. Can be negative, representing a refund due.
fields.sourceRef: T_Tax_Computation!C31, line 14 label at B31 — formula =ROUND(TaxComp.TotTaxableIncomeLessRlfS,2)+ROUND(TaxComp.TotTaxableIncomeLessRlfW,2)-ROUND(CreditIncome.TotalTaxCreditS,2)-ROUND(CreditIncome.TotalTaxCreditW,2).
